message.reasoning_content:
We need to write a Go source file with package topk, containing function TopKFrequent(words []string, k int) []string meeting requirements.

We need to avoid sorting all occurrences; we can use a min-heap of size k to keep top k elements based on frequency and lexical order. Steps:

- Count frequencies using map[string]int.
- If k <= 0, return empty slice (non-nil). Also if words empty, return empty slice.
- Use a heap (container/heap) with custom type that implements heap.Interface. The heap will be a min-heap where the "less" function defines that element a is less than b if:
   - freq[a] < freq[b] OR (freq equal && word a > word b) because we want to pop the smallest frequency, and for ties we want the lexicographically larger word to be considered smaller (since we want to keep lexicographically smaller words in top). Actually we need to keep top k by descending freq, ascending lexical. So min-heap should keep the worst among current top k at root, so we can compare new candidate: if candidate is better (higher freq, or same freq but smaller lexical) than root, we replace.

Define struct Item { word string; freq int }.

Define type MinHeap []Item.

Less(i,j) returns true if heap[i] is "worse" than heap[j] (i.e., lower freq, or same freq and larger word). So root is worst.

Implementation:

func (h MinHeap) Len() int { return len(h) }
func (h MinHeap) Less(i,j int) bool {
    if h[i].freq != h[j].freq {
        return h[i].freq < h[j].freq // lower freq is less (worse)
    }
    return h[i].word > h[j].word // lexicographically larger is less (worse)
}
func (h MinHeap) Swap(i,j int) { h[i], h[j] = h[j], h[i] }

Push/Pop using pointer receiver.

After building heap: iterate over map entries. For each item:
- if heap size < k: push.
- else if item is better than heap[0] (i.e., !Less(item, heap[0])? Actually we need to compare: if item is better (higher freq, or same freq and smaller word) than root, then replace root: heap[0] = item; heap.Fix(0).

Define a function better(a,b Item) bool: returns true if a is better than b (higher freq, or same freq and smaller word). So if better(item, heap[0]) then replace.

After processing, we have heap of up to k items, but not sorted. Need to output slice sorted by descending freq, ascending lexical. So we can extract items into slice, sort using sort.Slice with comparator: higher freq first, if equal lower lexical.

Return slice of words.

Edge Cases: k may be larger than number of unique words; we just return all unique words sorted accordingly.

Complexities: O(N + U log k) where U unique words.

Make sure not to mutate input slice; we only read.

Return non-nil empty slice: make([]string,0).

Now produce Go file.

Need to import container/heap, sort.

Write package topk.

Write function TopKFrequent.

Write internal types.

Make sure to comment.

Now produce final answer.
